Interface KeyboardEvent.KeyEvent

All Superinterfaces:
Event, InputEvent, KeyboardEvent
All Known Implementing Classes:
KeyboardEvent.KeyPressedEvent, KeyboardEvent.KeyReleasedEvent, KeyboardEvent.KeyRepeatedEvent
Enclosing interface:
KeyboardEvent
public static non-sealed interface KeyboardEvent.KeyEvent extends KeyboardEvent
Base interface for events representing a physical key input. This event can be used to detect function keys.

Important note: This interface should NOT be used to input a character since physical keys can not be mapped 1:1 to characters. The character value depends on the keyboard layout, modifier keys held, etc. Most function keys do not even have a character representation. Use CharInputEvent for that purpose.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface KeyboardEvent

    KeyboardEvent.CharInputEvent, KeyboardEvent.KeyEvent, KeyboardEvent.KeyPressedEvent, KeyboardEvent.KeyReleasedEvent, KeyboardEvent.KeyRepeatedEvent

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    MOD_ALT
    If this bit is set in modifiers, one or more Alt (AKA Option on Mac) keys were held down.
    static final int
    MOD_CAPS_LOCK
    If this bit is set in modifiers, the Caps Lock key is enabled.
    static final int
    MOD_CONTROL
    If this bit is set in modifiers, one or more Control keys were held down.
    static final int
    MOD_META
    If this bit is set in modifiers, one or more Meta (AKA Windows on Windows, Super on Linux, Command on Mac) keys were held down.
    static final int
    MOD_NUM_LOCK
    If this bit is set in modifiers, the Num Lock key is enabled.
    static final int
    MOD_SHIFT
    If this bit is set in modifiers, one or more Shift keys were held down.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default boolean
    isAltDown()
    Returns whether one of the Alt keys was held down.
    default boolean
    isCapsLockOn()
    Returns whether Caps Lock was enabled.
    default boolean
    isControlDown()
    Returns whether one of the Control keys was held down.
    default boolean
    isMetaDown()
    Returns whether one of the Meta keys was held down.
    default boolean
    isNumLockOn()
    Returns whether Num Lock was enabled.
    default boolean
    isShiftDown()
    Returns whether one of the Shift keys was held down.
    Key
    key()
    Returns the physical key this event is associated with.
    int
    modifiers()
    Returns the bitfield describing which modifier keys were held down.

  • Method Details

    • key

      Key key()
      Returns the physical key this event is associated with.
      Returns:
      the key

    • modifiers

      int modifiers()
      Returns the bitfield describing which modifier keys were held down.

      Example of checking if a modifier was present: 

          // ...
    int mod = event.modifiers();
    if ((mod & KeyEvent.MOD_SHIFT) != 0) {
        // do something if the Shift key was down
    }
    if ((mod & KeyEvent.MOD_NUM_LOCK) != 0) {
        // do something if Num Lock was enabled
    }
    if ((mod & KeyEvent.MOD_ALT) == KeyEvent.MOD_ALT) {
        // do something if ONLY the Alt/Option key was down
    }
    // ...
      Returns:
      the modifier keys that were held/enabled
      See Also:
      • MOD_SHIFT
      • MOD_CONTROL
      • MOD_ALT
      • MOD_META
      • MOD_CAPS_LOCK
      • MOD_NUM_LOCK

    • isShiftDown

      default boolean isShiftDown()
      Returns whether one of the Shift keys was held down.

      whether Shift was held down
